title: bridge-matrix: hs_token constant-time compare leaks length via short-circuit
description: as_transaction (bridge.rs:151-153) does expected.len() == provided.len() && bool::from(expected.ct_eq(provided)). The length check short-circuits before ct_eq: an attacker probing tokens of different lengths gets a faster rejection than tokens of the correct length. Length is leaked in timing. The hs_token is a random bearer token; timing on length narrows brute-force from all-lengths to a single length. Use a crate-level constant-time comparison that handles unequal lengths internally (e.g. subtle::ct_eq on zero-padded inputs, or ring::constant_time::verify_slices_are_equal).
